instructions: Heat oil or ghee in a wide karahi or heavy pan over high heat.
Add cumin seeds (optional) and let them sizzle.
Add chicken pieces and sauté until they turn white and lightly golden.
Add crushed garlic and ginger; cook for 2–3 minutes until aromatic.
Add chopped tomatoes, salt, red chili powder, coriander powder, and black pepper.
Cook on high heat, stirring frequently, until tomatoes break down and form a thick gravy (10–15 minutes).
Add green chilies and continue cooking until oil separates from the masala.
Optionally stir in yogurt for a richer, slightly creamy texture.
Reduce heat and simmer for 5–8 minutes until chicken is fully tender and coated in thick masala.
Garnish with fresh coriander and extra ginger slices before serving.
serving_suggestion: Serve hot with naan, roti, or steamed basmati rice.
notes: Authentic karahi relies on high heat and minimal water for a concentrated flavor.
Tomatoes are the primary source of gravy—no need for added water unless necessary.
For stronger street-style flavor, increase green chilies and ginger.
